candidates.why: Clearly labeled top-line revenue; appropriate primary choice for total revenue/sales.
Income from associates is below-EBIT income, not operating revenue; not suitable for REVT.
Equity-accounted income is non-operating; not core revenue.
Represents financial income, not operating revenue from customers.
Represents total comprehensive income, not revenue.

variables.notes: Total Revenue is the clearly indicated top-line sales figure and is the appropriate REVT choice.

candidates.why: Mixed other income/expense, not direct production cost; unsuitable for COGS.
Non-recurring, non-business items; not cost of goods sold.
Direct material and consumables cost; best representation of cost of goods sold.
Personnel costs that may include both production and overhead; less direct than raw materials for COGS.
Broad operating expenses bucket likely including overhead; too aggregated for strict COGS.
Net operating figure, not a pure cost-of-sales line.
Financing costs, not production costs.
Financial, not operational COGS.
Financial, not COGS.
Non-operating by definition; not COGS.
Operating overhead, not direct cost of goods in this context.
Mixed other operating items, not core COGS.
Inventory balance, not period expense; not a P&L COGS item.
Liability balance; not COGS.
Liability balance; not COGS.

variables.notes: Raw material and consumable used is the direct production cost line and best represents COGS.

candidates.why: Personnel costs are a core SG&A component when not included in COGS; suitable SG&A element here.
Broad operating overhead bucket; appropriate SG&A component in absence of explicit SG&A line.
Mixed operating income/expenses; less clearly pure SG&A, so lower priority.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A line is present; using main overhead buckets Salaries and personnel expenses and Operating expenses as SG&A components, excluding totals and depreciation/impairment lines.
No research & development-related line items are visible in the provided income statement; treating XRD as structurally absent (0).

candidates.why: Primary interest expense line; best representation of financing cost.

variables.notes: Using the main Interest Expense line first, with lease interest lines as fallbacks, following the priority rule for XINT.

candidates.why: Income statement item, not an equity balance; not suitable for BE.

candidates.row_label: Proft to equity holder of parent from discontinuing operations
candidates.why: Discontinued operations profit allocation; not the equity stock.
First Total Equity line (17078 etc.) corresponds to equity attributable to owners (before adding non-controlling interests); appropriate BE choice.
Technical balancing row, not the substantive equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Technical balancing row; not pure equity.
Represents total assets/liabilities+equity; not equity alone.

variables.notes: The balance sheet shows owners’ equity (Total Equity of 17078 etc.) plus a separate Non-controlling interests line; mapping BE to Total Equity (owners’ portion) and MIB to Non-controlling interests avoids double counting.

candidates.why: Income allocation to NCI; helpful for P&L but not the balance sheet MIB stock.
Comprehensive income allocated to minorities; again income, not equity stock.
Relates to owners, not minorities; not suitable for MIB stock.

candidates.row_label: Profit to controlling interests excl extritems
candidates.why: Owners’ income; not MIB.
Refers to majority (controlling) interests, not minorities.
Explicit balance sheet line for minority equity; ideal mapping for MIB.
Alternative label for the same non-controlling equity; good fallback.

variables.notes: Using the balance sheet Non-controlling interests line (with Minority Interest as a fallback) to capture the minority equity stock for MIB.
